framework.name: ISO/IEC 27017:2015 — Cloud Services Security
framework.description: Code of practice for information security controls based on ISO/IEC 27002 for cloud services. Provides cloud-specific guidance on ISO 27002 controls and introduces 7 new cloud-specific controls (CLD prefix).

pending_update.note: Second edition is at Final Draft International Standard stage. Will align control structure to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 (four-theme layout: Organisational/People/Physical/Technological), replacing the current 2015 edition which follows ISO/IEC 27002:2013 clause numbering. Dataset requires full rewrite on publication.

objects.title: Cloud-Specific Controls
objects.description: Seven new controls introduced by ISO 27017 that do not exist in ISO 27002. Applicable to both cloud service providers (CSPs) and cloud service customers (CSCs).

objects.title: Cloud Service Provider Guidance
objects.description: Cloud-specific implementation guidance for ISO 27002 controls from the cloud service provider perspective.

objects.title: Cloud Service Customer Guidance
objects.description: Cloud-specific implementation guidance for ISO 27002 controls from the cloud service customer perspective.

objects.title: Shared roles and responsibilities within a cloud computing environment
objects.description: The allocation and division of information security responsibilities between the cloud service provider and the cloud service customer shall be documented, communicated, and implemented. Responsibility matrices (e.g. RACI) shall be established for all security controls to avoid gaps or overlaps.

objects.title: Removal and return of cloud service customer assets
objects.description: Upon termination or expiry of a cloud service agreement, the cloud service provider shall return or securely delete all cloud service customer assets (data, configurations, artefacts) within a defined period. The process and timeline shall be contractually specified.

objects.title: Segregation in virtual computing environments
objects.description: Virtual computing environments shared between multiple cloud service customers shall be segregated to prevent unauthorised access, data leakage, and resource interference. Hypervisor and container isolation controls shall be implemented and tested.

objects.title: Virtual machine hardening
objects.description: Virtual machine images used to instantiate cloud services shall be hardened and shall contain only the necessary components. Base images shall be maintained, versioned, and patched. Unused services and ports on virtual machines shall be disabled.

objects.title: Administrator's operational security
objects.description: Cloud service providers shall implement controls to protect against risks associated with administrator access including: privileged access management, separation of duties, monitoring of admin activity, just-in-time access, and multi-factor authentication for all administrative interfaces.

objects.title: Monitoring of cloud services
objects.description: The cloud service provider shall monitor the cloud service and provide monitoring capabilities to the cloud service customer. Monitoring shall cover availability, performance, security events, and compliance metrics. Customers shall have access to logs relevant to their tenancy.

objects.title: Alignment of security management for virtual and physical networks
objects.description: Security controls applied to physical networks shall be consistently applied to virtual networks within the cloud environment. Network segmentation, firewall rules, and traffic inspection policies shall be enforced equivalently in virtual network layers.

objects.title: Information security roles and responsibilities (cloud)
objects.description: Cloud service providers shall define, document, and communicate information security roles and responsibilities applicable to the cloud service. Service level agreements shall include security responsibilities and escalation paths.

objects.title: Capacity and availability management (cloud)
objects.description: Cloud service providers shall monitor, plan, and manage capacity to ensure agreed service availability. Customers shall be informed of capacity constraints that may affect agreed service levels.

objects.title: Incident response notification (cloud)
objects.description: Cloud service providers shall notify cloud service customers of security incidents that affect their data or services within agreed timeframes. Incident notifications shall include: nature of incident, affected data, actions taken, and customer obligations.

objects.title: Access control in cloud environments
objects.description: Cloud service customers shall implement identity and access management controls for cloud resources including: least privilege, role-based access, MFA, and regular access reviews. IAM configurations shall be reviewed whenever personnel changes occur.

objects.title: Data protection in cloud services
objects.description: Cloud service customers shall implement controls to protect data in transit and at rest within cloud environments. Data classification shall determine applicable encryption and access control requirements. Data residency requirements shall be contractually enforced.

controls.title: Shared roles and responsibilities within a cloud computing environment
controls.description: Roles and responsibilities of cloud service providers and cloud service customers shall be defined, documented and communicated. Both parties must understand who is responsible for each security function within the shared responsibility model.
controls.notes: New control — not present in ISO 27002. Unique to cloud environments.

controls.title: Removal and return of assets upon contract termination
controls.description: When a cloud service contract ends, the cloud service provider shall return or securely destroy the cloud service customer's assets. Procedures for asset return, secure deletion, and data remanence prevention shall be agreed contractually.
controls.notes: New control — not present in ISO 27002.

controls.title: Segregation in virtual computing environments
controls.description: Virtual computing environments shall be protected and segregated to prevent unauthorised access between cloud service customers. Isolation mechanisms shall ensure one customer cannot access another customer's environment or data.
controls.notes: New control — not present in ISO 27002.

controls.title: Virtual machine hardening
controls.description: Virtual machine images shall be hardened in accordance with organisation policy before deployment. Hardening shall cover removal of unnecessary services, application of security baselines, and validation of configuration integrity.
controls.notes: New control — not present in ISO 27002.

controls.title: Administrator's operational security
controls.description: Administrative operations and procedures in cloud environments shall be documented and applied consistently. Privileged administrative access shall be controlled, logged and regularly reviewed to prevent unauthorised operations.
controls.notes: New control — not present in ISO 27002.

controls.title: Monitoring of cloud services
controls.description: Cloud service customers shall have the ability to monitor their use of cloud services. Providers shall make available sufficient logging and monitoring data to allow customers to verify security status and investigate incidents.
controls.notes: New control — not present in ISO 27002.

controls.title: Alignment of security management for virtual and physical networks
controls.description: Security management for virtual networks shall be aligned with physical network security policies and procedures. Network segregation, access control, and monitoring shall apply consistently across physical and virtual network boundaries.
controls.notes: New control — not present in ISO 27002.

controls.title: Information security roles and responsibilities in cloud environments
controls.description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated with explicit consideration of cloud-specific functions, including responsibilities under the shared responsibility model for the cloud deployment type in use.
controls.notes: Cloud-specific implementation of ISO 27002 organisational controls.

controls.title: Access control policy for cloud service customers
controls.description: Cloud service customers shall implement access control policies appropriate for cloud environments, including identity and access management for cloud resources, privileged access management, and federated identity where applicable.
controls.notes: Cloud-specific implementation of ISO 27002 access control.

controls.title: Capacity and availability management for cloud services
controls.description: Cloud service providers shall manage capacity to ensure agreed service availability levels. Resource allocation, elasticity mechanisms, and availability monitoring shall be implemented and communicated to cloud service customers.
controls.notes: Cloud-specific implementation of ISO 27002 operations security.

controls.title: Notification of security incidents affecting cloud customers
controls.description: Cloud service providers shall notify affected cloud service customers of security incidents without undue delay. Notification shall include sufficient information for customers to assess impact and take protective action on their own systems.
controls.notes: Cloud-specific implementation of ISO 27002 incident management.

controls.title: Protection of data in cloud services
controls.description: Cloud service customers shall identify and implement appropriate controls to protect data stored in and transmitted to/from cloud services, including encryption, data residency controls, and secure deletion procedures.
controls.notes: Cloud-specific implementation of ISO 27002 communications security.
